summary_quote: Aim alignment: 2/10. Scope: on-scope. Verdict: rescope. Pipeline falsifies 3 of 4 stated success criteria (reproducibility, no-leakage split, final test accuracy) — nothing produced can be trusted against the user's own bar.

findings.title: Pipeline regresses three of four stated success criteria; the work as-shipped cannot satisfy the user's stated bar
findings.explanation: Aim alignment: 2/10. Scope: on-scope. Verdict: rescope. The Goal in aims.md is 'train a baseline MLP on tabular data with reproducible runs and trustworthy metrics.' The PR delivers a training loop that runs to completion, but it actively falsifies the user's own definition of success on three of four criteria from aims.md:9-14. Per-criterion accounting: (1) 'Training is reproducible' — REGRESSED. No seeds set anywhere; data generated via np.random.randn with no seed; DataLoader has shuffle=True with no generator. (2) 'Train/val/test split exists and there is no leakage' — REGRESSED. load_full_dataset returns the entire dataset; train() feeds this single dataset into one loader. The source file's own docstring acknowledges this. (3) 'Metrics are logged per epoch' — PARTIAL. Train loss is printed per epoch but val loss is never computed and metrics live in stdout only. (4) 'A test set evaluation runs at the end and produces a single accuracy number' — NOT TOUCHED. train() exits after the loss-printing loop; there is no test-loader pass, no accuracy computation, no final number. Scope is on-scope — the PR is doing the right kind of work in the right files. Non-goals are respected. The issue is execution: the user defined four measurable criteria; the code meets one partially and contradicts three.
findings.suggestion: Hold for a rescope PR before any further iteration. Minimum sequence to close the criteria (~55-70 lines total): (1) add a set_seed(seed: int) helper covering random.seed, np.random.seed, torch.manual_seed, torch.cuda.manual_seed_all, and torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = True; thread seed through TabularDataset and DataLoader; (2) replace load_full_dataset with load_splits(seed) returning (train, val, test) tensors with disjoint indices; (3) compute val loss per epoch alongside train loss and append to a metrics JSON or CSV; (4) after the epoch loop, run the model in inference mode over the test loader once and print a single final accuracy. After these four changes land, every criterion from aims.md:9-14 becomes addressable.

findings.title: Highest-leverage work is reproducibility + split + final-eval, not the perf optimizations or code-style nits
findings.explanation: Prior findings span code style (print vs logging, type hints, magic config keys, deprecated decorator), performance (num_workers, set_to_none, tensor regen), and correctness against aims (seeds, splits, val loss, final accuracy). For this phase, only the last bucket moves stated criteria. Sequencing recommendation: (a) close the three regressed criteria first via the rescope PR; (b) then a follow-up that addresses DataLoader num_workers and set_to_none findings (these matter for the 30-minute training-time constraint but only once the pipeline produces trustworthy outputs); (c) then readability findings as polish.
findings.suggestion: Do not merge optimization or style PRs from this scope before the rescope PR lands. The training-time constraint (under 30 minutes) is not yet at risk on a single-GPU desktop with this model size — defer perf work until correctness is in place.

findings.title: Existing test coverage cannot detect regressions on any stated success criterion
findings.explanation: The only existing test asserts the MLP constructor returns non-None — it cannot detect a regression on any of the four criteria. If reproducibility is fixed today and someone removes the seed call tomorrow, no test will catch it. peer-quality-engineer flagged this independently (score 3). In the rescope PR, adding minimal regression tests would convert the four criteria from 'currently met' to 'protected against regression'.
findings.suggestion: Bundle three small regression tests into the rescope PR: (1) test_reproducible_first_batch_loss — run train() twice with seed=42 for one epoch, assert epoch-1 train loss matches to 1e-6; (2) test_splits_disjoint — call load_splits(seed=42), assert no index overlap between train/val/test; (3) test_final_accuracy_returned — train() returns a float in [0, 1]. Without these, the criteria can be silently broken by future PRs.

stage_handoff_notes: Aims are clear and well-captured; no rescoping of aims.md itself is recommended. The architect may surface a structural critique (separate Experiment boundary) — that is their lens and independent of alignment grading; the alignment fix is closing the four criteria, which can be done in the existing single-file shape if the user prefers minimal change. Non-goals (distributed training, hyperparameter search, serving) are respected and should remain so in the rescope PR. If the user disagrees with the block verdict — for example, they want to merge this as a checkpoint — recommend updating aims.md to mark reproducibility/split/final-eval as 'next phase' criteria, so the alignment grade reflects revised intent.
